title: Block 5 Test: Handling Emergencies
learningGoal: Evaluate your ability to handle medical and legal emergencies in Hindi.
instructions: This test covers the vocabulary, grammar, and situations related to medical emergencies and police reports. You must score at least 80% to pass this block.

questions.questionText: What is the Hindi word for 'Hospital'?
What does 'चक्कर' (Chakkar) mean?
Choose the correct translation for 'Complaint':
What does 'बटुआ' (Batua) mean?
Which word means 'Accident'?

sections.sectionTitle: Grammar & Structure: Describing Symptoms and Incidents

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank: _____ बहुत तेज़ सिरदर्द है। (I have a very severe headache.)
How do you say 'My bag has been stolen' using the passive voice?
options: मैंने बैग चोरी किया। (Maine bag chori kiya.)
मेरा बैग चोरी हो गया है। (Mera bag chori ho gaya hai.)
मुझे बैग चोरी है। (Mujhe bag chori hai.)

questionHints.targetText: मेरा बैग चोरी हो गया है।
questionHints.transliteration: Mera bag chori ho gaya hai.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank: मेरे पेट ___ दर्द है। (I have pain in my stomach.)
Which is the correct way to ask a doctor about the medicine dosage?
options: दवा कितनी बार खानी चाहिए? (Dawa kitni baar khaani chahiye?)

questionHints.baseTranslation: How many times should the medicine be taken?

questions.questionText: What verb means 'to register / file' (as in a complaint)?
If you witness an accident and want to call an ambulance, what should you say?
options: मुझे अस्पताल जाना है। (Mujhe aspataal jaana hai.)
मेरा बैग चोरी हो गया है। (Mera bag chori ho gaya hai.)

questions.questionText: At the police station, what number do you dial in India for police assistance?
You want to say 'Please help me, I am lost.' Which translation is correct?
options: कृपया मेरी मदद कीजिए, मैं बीमार हूँ। (Kripya meri madad kijiye, main beemar hoon.)
कृपया मेरी मदद कीजिए, मैं खो गया हूँ। (Kripya meri madad kijiye, main kho gaya hoon.)
कृपया मुझे पानी दीजिए। (Kripya mujhe paani dijiye.)
मैं घर जाना चाहता हूँ। (Main ghar jaana chahta hoon.)

questionHints.targetText: कृपया मेरी मदद कीजिए, मैं खो गया हूँ।
questionHints.transliteration: Kripya meri madad kijiye, main kho gaya hoon.
questionHints.baseTranslation: Please help me, I am lost.

questions.questionText: How do you tell a doctor you've had a fever since yesterday?
options: मुझे कल बुखार था। (Mujhe kal bukhar tha.)
मुझे कल रात से बुखार है। (Mujhe kal raat se bukhar hai.)
मुझे बुखार नहीं है। (Mujhe bukhar nahi hai.)
मेरा बुखार ठीक है। (Mera bukhar theek hai.)

questionHints.targetText: मुझे कल रात से बुखार है।
questionHints.transliteration: Mujhe kal raat se bukhar hai.
questionHints.baseTranslation: I have a fever since last night.

questions.questionText: If someone asks 'क्या हुआ आपके साथ?' (What happened with you?), and your wallet was stolen, you reply:
options: मेरा बटुआ चोरी हो गया है। (Mera batua chori ho gaya hai.)
मुझे चक्कर आ रहे हैं। (Mujhe chakkar aa rahe hain.)
मैं खो गया हूँ। (Main kho gaya hoon.)
मुझे पुलिस पसंद है। (Mujhe police pasand hai.)

questionHints.targetText: मेरा बटुआ चोरी हो गया है।
questionHints.transliteration: Mera batua chori ho gaya hai.

questions.questionText: Read: 'स्टेशन पर बहुत भीड़ थी। अचानक, मैंने देखा कि मेरा बैग गायब था।' What happened?
options: The person found a bag in the crowd.
The person noticed their bag was missing in the crowd.
The person was waiting for a friend.

questionHints.targetText: स्टेशन पर बहुत भीड़ थी। अचानक, मैंने देखा कि मेरा बैग गायब था।
questionHints.transliteration: Station par bahut bheed thi. Achanak, maine dekha ki mera bag gaayab tha.
questionHints.baseTranslation: There was a lot of crowd at the station. Suddenly, I saw that my bag was missing.

questions.questionText: Read: 'डॉक्टर ने कहा: यह गोली दिन में दो बार खाइए।' What is the doctor's instruction?
options: Take the pill once a day.
Take the pill twice a day.

questionHints.targetText: डॉक्टर ने कहा: यह गोली दिन में दो बार खाइए।
questionHints.transliteration: Doctor ne kaha: Yeh goli din mein do baar khaiye.
questionHints.baseTranslation: The doctor said: Take this pill twice a day.

questions.questionText: Read: 'तनाव के कारण उसे तेज़ सिरदर्द होने लगा।' Why did the person get a headache?

questionHints.targetText: तनाव के कारण उसे तेज़ सिरदर्द होने लगा।
questionHints.transliteration: Tanaav ke kaaran use tez sirdard hone laga.
questionHints.baseTranslation: Due to stress, he started having a severe headache.

questions.questionText: Read: 'मैंने पुलिस स्टेशन में एफ.आई.आर. (F.I.R.) दर्ज की।' What did the person do?
options: Filed an FIR at the police station.

questionHints.targetText: मैंने पुलिस स्टेशन में एफ.आई.आर. (F.I.R.) दर्ज की।
questionHints.transliteration: Maine police station mein F.I.R. darj ki.
questionHints.baseTranslation: I filed an F.I.R. at the police station.

questions.questionText: Translate this sentence to English: 'मेरे बटुए में मेरा पहचान पत्र था।' (Mere batue mein mera pehchaan patra tha.)
options: There was money in my wallet.
My identity card was in my wallet.
My passport was in my wallet.

questionHints.targetText: मेरे बटुए में मेरा पहचान पत्र था।
questionHints.transliteration: Mere batue mein mera pehchaan patra tha.
questionHints.baseTranslation: My identity card was in my wallet.

answerKey.q7: मेरा बैग चोरी हो गया है। (Mera bag chori ho gaya hai.)
answerKey.q9: दवा कितनी बार खानी चाहिए? (Dawa kitni baar khaani chahiye?)
answerKey.q13: कृपया मेरी मदद कीजिए, मैं खो गया हूँ। (Kripya meri madad kijiye, main kho gaya hoon.)
answerKey.q14: मुझे कल रात से बुखार है। (Mujhe kal raat se bukhar hai.)
answerKey.q15: मेरा बटुआ चोरी हो गया है। (Mera batua chori ho gaya hai.)
answerKey.q16: The person noticed their bag was missing in the crowd.
answerKey.q17: Take the pill twice a day.
answerKey.q19: Filed an FIR at the police station.
answerKey.q20: My identity card was in my wallet.

feedbackRules.onPass: Congratulations! You have successfully completed Block 5. You are now prepared to handle emergencies in India.
feedbackRules.onFail: You scored below 80%. Please review the block materials, especially the vocabulary for medical and legal situations.
feedbackRules.onPartial: You are almost there. Review your mistakes and try the test again.

hintText: Read the questions carefully and remember the 'Mujhe + Symptom' rule.
